# DAST conventions — runtime security testing (Layer 4 of `docs/dast-plan.md`)

Every static control (Semgrep/OSV/Trivy/Gitleaks/ASVS) analyzes code **at rest**; DAST attacks the
**running** app. This skill is the planning-time half: it makes an app *arrive* at each DAST layer
already carrying what that layer needs, so a missing schema or test user is a **plan-audit material
flag today**, not a broken scan job later.

## The layers and what each honestly guarantees

| Layer | What runs | Guarantee | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| 1 — ZAP passive baseline | post-GREEN, local Docker, opt-in via `.pipeline/dast.env` | response-observable classes only (headers, cookies, info leaks) — **advisory, never a gate** | built (PR #26) |
| 2 — Schemathesis API fuzz | CI, against staging, driven by the OpenAPI schema | every documented endpoint fuzzed for 5xx/contract violations; **needs the schema + test user this skill's ACs guarantee** | spec (needs staging) |
| 3 — ZAP active scan (authenticated) | nightly vs staging, never a shared env | real attack payloads with a session context; gates on High | spec |
| 4 — this skill + the planning ACs | planning time | the readiness contract below | built |

Promotion rule: Layer 1 is advisory locally; when re-homed into CI (`pipeline-ci.yml`'s reserved
`dast-baseline` job) it may go **blocking-on-High** — never stricter than High, and only in CI.

## DAST-readiness acceptance criteria (planning emits these for any served HTTP surface)

Emit into `acceptance.md`, each as a normal criterion riding `criteria_covered`:

- **DAST-1 — served OpenAPI schema.** The app serves (or commits) an OpenAPI document that matches
  the implemented routes (FastAPI/NestJS generate one for free — the criterion is that it exists,
  is reachable in non-prod, and isn't hand-drifted). This is Layer 2's driver: an endpoint absent
  from the schema is an endpoint the fuzzer never visits.
- **DAST-2 — seeded, non-production DAST test user** *(only when endpoints are authenticated)*:
  a documented seed path (fixture/script) creates a low-privilege test principal in staging/local;
  credentials come from env/secrets, never hardcoded.
- **DAST-3 — auth-context config** *(with DAST-2)*: how a scanner logs in as that user (login
  endpoint + token shape, or session cookie name) recorded in the plan so ZAP/Schemathesis auth
  can be configured without reverse-engineering.

Skip DAST-2/3 with a one-line N/A when the API is wholly unauthenticated; skip all three when the
feature serves no HTTP surface. plan-audit material-flags a served-HTTP plan missing DAST-1 (and
DAST-2/3 when auth exists).

## Opt-in mechanics (operator, per project)

Copy `templates/dast.env` → `.pipeline/dast.env` (target URL + boot command; kept untracked —
git-tracked copies are refused, `smoke.env` posture) and optionally `templates/dast-budget.json` →
`.pipeline/dast-budget.json` (per-severity caps; safe defaults when absent). No `dast.env` or no
Docker ⇒ the stage no-ops fail-safe.

## Tuning / false-positive protocol

- Tune by **budget**, not by silencing: raise the relevant severity cap in `dast-budget.json` with
  a one-line reason comment in the PR — visible in the diff, human-reviewed.
- A finding that is genuinely N/A for the stack (e.g. a CSP alert on a pure JSON API) may be
  ignored via ZAP's rule-ignore config **committed to the repo** — same posture as
  `.semgrepignore`/`.trivyignore`: committed, visible, reviewed. Never local-only suppression.
- A recurring-noise rule is a signal to fix the response surface (headers middleware in
  `api-edge-conventions`), not to grow the ignore file.
- Layer 1 results are advisory; treat a new High as a real finding to triage before merge even
  though the gate doesn't force it.
